name: MITRE.LIN.T1055.RULE Process Injection
notes:
The following auditd rules should be enabled:

-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S ptrace -k siem-process-injection
-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S ptrace -F a0=0x4 -k siem-process-injection
-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S ptrace -F a0=0x5 -k siem-process-injection
-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S ptrace -F a0=0x6 -k siem-process-injection

Get more Linux MITRE rules: https://www.scnsoft.com/services/security/siem/linux-mitre-attack-rules
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
when the event matches Linux Auditd Key (custom) is any of siem-process-injection
when any of Linux UID (custom) are contained in any of MITRE: Linux Users - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
TD_list: APPLY MITRE.LIN.T1055.RULE Process Injection on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
AND when the event matches Linux Auditd Key (custom) is any of siem-process-injection
AND NOT when any of Linux UID (custom) are contained in any of MITRE: Linux Users - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
RED_list:
Event description: Execution via process injection may also evade detection from security products since the execution is masked under a legitimate process.
See more on: https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1055
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
Include detected events by "Source IP" from this point forward, in the offense, for 0 second(s)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
